questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A missile is fired for maximum range with a initial velocity of $20 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. If $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$, the range of the missile is
questions.solution:
For maximum range of projectile $\theta$ will be $45^{\circ}$ by the law of projectile motion
$\therefore \quad R_{\max }=\frac{u^2}{g}$
Given, $\quad u=20 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ and $g=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$
$\begin{aligned}
& R_{\max }=\frac{(20)^2}{10}=\frac{400}{10} \\
& R_{\max }=40 \mathrm{~m}
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two boys are standing at the ends $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ of a ground where $\mathrm{AB}=a$. The boy at $\mathrm{B}$ starts running in a direction perpendicular to $\mathrm{AB}$ with velocity $v_1$. The boy at A starts running simultaneously with velocity $v$ and catches the other in time $t$, where $t$ is:
questions.solution:
If boy \(A\) catches boy \(B\) in time \(t\),
\(\begin{aligned}
& \Rightarrow(\mathrm{vt})^2=\left(\mathrm{v}_1 \mathrm{t}\right)^2+\mathrm{a}^2 \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^2=\frac{\mathrm{a}^2}{\mathrm{v}^2-\mathrm{v}_1^2} \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}=\frac{\mathrm{a}}{\sqrt{\mathrm{v}^2-\mathrm{v}_1^2}}
\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Four persons K, L, M, N are initially at the four corners of a square of side d. Each person now moves with a uniform speed   v in such a way that K always moves directly towards L, L directly towards M, M directly towards N, and N directly towards K. The four persons will meet at a time
options: The four persons will never meet
questions.solution:
The velocity of K throughout the motion towards the centre of the square is   v    cos45° and the displacement covered by this velocity will be KO.

  ∴ t= KO vcos45° = ( 2 d 2 ) v 2 = d v
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A fighter plane enters inside the enemy territory, at time t=0 with velocity v0=250 m s-1 and moves horizontally with constant acceleration a=20 m s-2 (see figure). An enemy tank, at the border, spots the plane and fire shots at an angle θ=60°with the horizontal and with velocityu=600 m s-1.The altitude H, where the plane can be hit by the shot, is
questions.solution:
If being hit, then horizontal distance remains same.



vx=600 cos 60ο=300 m s-1

250 m s-1t+1220t2=300 t

50 t = 1 2 20 t 2

t = 0   or   t = 5

H = 600 sin 60 ο t - 1 2 g t 2

   =60032×5-12·1025

H=30035-125 m

H=2473 m

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle moves along a circle $\left(\frac{20}{\pi}\right) \mathrm{m}$ with constant tangential acceleration. If the velocity of the particle is $80 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ at the end of the second revolution after after motion has begun, the tangential acceleration is:
questions.solution:
From question
$\begin{aligned}
& r=\frac{20}{\pi} m, v=80 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& \therefore \theta=2 \text { revolutions }=2 \times 2 \pi=4 \pi \mathrm{rad} \\
& \therefore \omega^2=\omega_0{ }^2+2 \alpha \theta \\
& \therefore \omega^2=2 \alpha \theta \ldots(\mathrm{i}) \text { since } \omega_0=0 \\
& \text { Since } \alpha=\frac{a}{r} \text { and } \omega=\frac{\mathrm{V}}{r}
\end{aligned}$
Putting these values in eq. (i)
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{V^2}{r^2}= \frac{2 a \theta}{r} \\
& \Rightarrow a= \frac{V^2}{2 r \theta}=\frac{80 \times 80}{2 \times\left(\frac{20}{\pi}\right) \times 4 \pi} \\
&=40 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If a person can throw a stone to maximum height of \(\mathrm{h}\) metre vertically, then the maximum distance through which it can be thrown horizontally by the same person is
questions.solution:
Hints:
\(\mathrm{h}=\frac{\mathrm{u}^2}{2 \mathrm{~g}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{u}^2=2 \mathrm{gh}\) Maximum horizontal distance \(\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_{\max }=\frac{\mathrm{u}^2}{\mathrm{~g}}\left(\text { when } \theta=45^{\circ}\right) \\ & \mathrm{R}_{\max }=2 \mathrm{~h} \end{aligned}\)
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.q: A ball is thrown with a speed <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>u</mi></math>, at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>θ</mi></math> with the horizontal. At the highest point of its motion, the strength of gravity is somehow doubled. Taking this change into account, the total time of flight of the projectile is

Time of ascent

t1=usinθg

Time of descent

t2=2H2g

t2=u2sin2θ2g2=usinθ2g

t=t1+t2=usinθg+usinθ2g

⇒usinθg2+12

questions.q: A stone is projected with a velocity <math><mn>20</mn><msqrt><mn>2</mn></msqrt><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> at an angle of <math><mn>45</mn><mo>°</mo></math> to the horizontal. The average velocity of stone during its motion from starting point to its maximum height is <math><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><msup><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></mrow><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>)</mo></math>
questions.solution:
Refer figure are when projectile is at A, then



OC = R 2 = 1 2 u 2 g sin 2 θ = 1 2 × 2 0 2 2 1 0 sin 2 × 4 5 ∘

= 40

AC = H = u 2 sin 2 θ 2 g = 2 0 2 2 2 × 1 0 sin 2 4 5 ∘

∴    Displacement, OA = OC 2 + CA 2 = 4 0 2 + 2 0 2

Time of projectile from O to A

= 1 2 2 u sin θ g = u sin θ 2 g = 2 0 2 sin 4 5 ∘ 1 0 = 2 s = 4 0 2 + 2 0 2 2 = 1 0 5 ms - 1

Average velocity = displacement time

= 4 0 2 + 2 0 2 2 = 1 0 5 ms - 1

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q:
A wheel starts rotating from rest at time $t=0$ with a angular acceleration of 50 radians $/ \mathrm{s}^2$. The angular acceleration $(\alpha)$ decreases to zero value after 5 seconds. During this interval, $\alpha$ varies according to the equation
$\alpha=\alpha_0\left(1-\frac{t}{5}\right)$
The angular velocity at $t=5 \mathrm{~s}$ will be
questions.solution:
$\alpha=\alpha_0\left(1-\frac{t}{5}\right)$
At $t=0, \alpha=\alpha_0 \quad \therefore \alpha_0=50 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}^2$
$\frac{d \omega}{d t}=\alpha_0\left(1-\frac{t}{5}\right)$
$\begin{aligned} & \therefore \int_0^\omega d \omega=\alpha_0 \int_0^5\left(1-\frac{t}{5}\right) d t \Rightarrow \omega=\alpha_0\left[t-\frac{t^2}{10}\right]_0^5 \\ & =50\left(5-\frac{25}{10}\right) \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}=125 \mathrm{rad} / \mathrm{s}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Two trains are moving in the same direction along parallel tracks. One of them is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>200</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> long travelling with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>20</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The second one is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>800</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math> long travelling with a velocity of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>7</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math>. The time taken for the first train to overtake the second is
questions.solution:
From the figure, the relative displacement is



srel=800+200m=1000 m

υrel=υ1-υ2=20-7.5 m s-1

=12.5 m s-1

∴t=srelυrel=100012.5=80 s
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A projectile has a maximum range of $16 \mathrm{~km}$. At the highest point of its motion, it explodes into two equal masses. One mass drops vertically downwards. The horizontal distance covered by the other mass from the time of explosion is
questions.solution:
Maximum range,
$R_{\max }=\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}=\frac{u^2}{g}=16 \mathrm{~km}$
Range is maximum when $\theta=45^{\circ}$ Initial momentum at the highest point
$=m u \cos 45^{\circ}=\frac{m u}{\sqrt{2}}$
After explosion, the projectile breaks into two equal masses. As one mass drops vertically downwards, hence its velocity and momentum is zero in the $x$-direction.
Hence, if $v$ be the velocity of second mass, then according to law of conservation of momentum,
$m u \cos 45^{\circ}=\frac{m}{2} v \quad \therefore \quad v=2 u \cos 45^{\circ}$
Horizontal distance covered from the time of explosion
$\begin{aligned} & =v \times \frac{T}{2}=v \times \frac{1}{2} \times \frac{2 u \sin 45^{\circ}}{g} \\ & =2 u \cos 45^{\circ} \times \frac{u \sin 45^{\circ}}{g} \\ & =\frac{u^2}{g} \times \sin 90^{\circ}=\frac{u^2}{g}=16 \mathrm{~km}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A man crosses a river in a boat. If he crosses the river in minimum time, he takes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>10</mn><mi>min</mi></math> with a drift <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>120</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi></math>. If he crosses the river taking the shortest path, he takes <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>12</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>5</mn><mi>min</mi></math>, find the width of the river.
questions.solution:
Let vr = velocity of river

vbr = velocity of river in still water and

w = width of river

Given,   tmin = 10 min

or   wvbr=10      ...(i)

Drift, in this case, will be,

x=vrt

∴    120=10vr     ...(ii)





The shortest path is taken when vb is along AB. In this case,

vb=vbr2-vr2

Now,      12.5=wvb=wvbr2-vr2         ...(iii)

Solving these three equations, we get

vbr = 20 m min-1,

vr = 12 m min-1

and   w= 200 m
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A small object is thrown at an angle $45^{\circ}$ to the horizontal with an initial velocity $\mathbf{v}_0$. The velocity is averaged for first $\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~s}$ and the magnitude of average velocity comes out to be same as that of initial velocity, i.e. $\left|\mathbf{v}_{\mathrm{o}}\right|$. The magnitude $\left|\mathbf{v}_0\right|$ will be (take, $g=10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$ )
questions.solution: Let object is at $B(x, y)$ after $t=\sqrt{2} \mathrm{~s}$ Then, $x=u_x \times t=v_0 \cos 45^{\circ} \times \sqrt{2}=v_0$ and $y=u_y t-\frac{1}{2} a_y t^2=v_0\left(\sin 45^{\circ}\right) \sqrt{2}-\frac{1}{2}(10) \times(\sqrt{2})^2$ $$ =\left(v_0-10\right) \mathrm{m} $$ Displacement $O B$ of particle is $$ O B=\sqrt{O A^2+A B^2}=\sqrt{v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2} $$ So, $\quad v_{\text {avg }}=\frac{O B}{t}=v_0$ or $O B=v_0 t$ $$ \begin{array}{lrl} \Rightarrow & \sqrt{v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2}=v_0 \sqrt{2} & \Rightarrow v_0^2+\left(v_0-10\right)^2=2 v_0^2 \\ \Rightarrow & v_0-10= \pm v_0 & 2 v_0=10 \Rightarrow v_0=5 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1} \end{array} $$
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: An inclined plane is located at angle α=53° to the horizontal. There is a hole at point B in the inclined plane as shown in the figure. A particle is projected along the plane with speed v0 at an angle β=37° to the horizontal in such a way so that it gets into the hole. Neglect any type of friction. Find the speed v0 (in m s-1) if h=1 m and l=8m.
questions.solution:
h=ltan⁡β-12gsin⁡αl2v02cos2⁡β
v0=gsin⁡αl22cos2⁡βltan⁡β-h
=10×45×8×82×45×458×34-1=9 m s-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A particle is projected with velocity <math><mn>2</mn><msqrt><mi mathvariant="normal">g</mi><mi>h</mi></msqrt></math> so that it just clears two walls of equal height <math><mi>h</mi></math>, which are at a distance of <math><mn>2</mn><mi>h</mi></math> from each other. What is the time interval of passing between the two walls?
questions.solution:
Let t be the time interval. Then,



2h=ux(t)

or ux=2ht ...(i)

Further, h=uyt-12gt2

or gt2-2uyt+2h=0

 t1=2uy+4uy2-8gh2g

and t2=2uy-4uy2-8gh2g

t=t1-t2=4uy2-8ghg

or uy2=g2t24+2gh     ...(ii)

Given, ux2+uy2=2gh2

 4h2t2+g2t24+2gh=4gh

g24t4-2ght2+4h2=0

t2=2gh±4g2h2-4g2h2g2/2=4hg

or t=2hg
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: Taking <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>X</mi></math>-axis along horizontal and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Y</mi></math>-axis along vertical, with what minimum speed must a particle be projected from the origin so that it is able to pass through a given point <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>(</mo><mn>30</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo>)</mo></math>? Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo> </mo><mo>=</mo><mo> </mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>
questions.solution:
Using equation of trajectory for a projectile motion :

y=xtan⁡θ-gx22u21+tan2⁡θ

For the projectile to pass through (30 m, 40 m) :

40 = 30 tan α - g (30) 2 2u 2 1 + tan 2 α

or      900 tan2 α - (6u2 tan α) + (900 + 8u2) = 0

For real value of α. The discriminant of a quadratic equation b2-4ac≥0

                     (6u2)2 ≥ 3600 (900 + 8u2)

or        (u4 - 800 u2) ≥ 9,00,00

or           (u2 - 400)2 ≥ (2,50,000)

or               u2 - 400 ≥ 500

or             u2 ≥ 900  or  u ≥ 30 m s-1
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: A projectile is launched with an initial speed of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>40</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></math> at an angle <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>30</mn><mo>°</mo></math> above the ground. The projectile lands on a hillside <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>.</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi></math> later. The net displacement from where the projectile was launched to where it hits the target is (Take <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>g</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>10</mn><mo> </mo><mi mathvariant="normal">m</mi><mo> </mo><msup><mi mathvariant="normal">s</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup></math>)
questions.solution:
Horizontal displacement covered, 

x=40cos30°×2=403 m

Vertical displacement covered, 

y=40sin30°×2+12-10×22=40-20=20 m

Hence, required displacement will be, 

x2+y2=2013 m
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
questions.q: If a stone is to hit at a point which is at a distance d away and at a height h above the point from where the stone starts, then what is the value of initial speed u, if the stone is launched at an angle θ?
questions.solution:
Using equation of motion for projectile in x and y-direction.

h=usinθt-12gt2

d=ucosθt or t=ducosθ

⟹h=usinθ.ducosθ-12g.d2u2cos2θ

⟹u=dcosθg2dtanθ-h
questions.source: Highly selective Backlog Qs for JEE Main
